note: seq=121: PER-SOURCE OUTPUT-FRESHNESS MONITOR (the real fix for NRD silently dying). Tracks per source: timer ENABLED+SCHEDULED (a disabled/unscheduled timer on a oneshot = failure even though service!='failed') AND actual PRODUCTION (max(business.first_seen) per source vs SLA). On stale/dead-timer -> AUTO-REVIVE (enable --now + start) + LOUD escalate (freshness.json + alert-freshness-*.json). Tier-1 prime birth feeds (NRD/SoS/CT) get tightest SLA (NRD 26h). Pure decision core assess() is selftest-proven 6/6 INCLUDING the exact NRD bug (enabled-but-not-scheduled+stale->revive). Step 4 = on-box --selftest GATE: manifest fails (auto-rollback) unless selftest exits 0. Verified-complete 142L,__main__,clean-end.

steps.note: seq=121: per-source output-freshness monitor; selftest-gated; auto-revive dead timers + stale producers.

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; p='/opt/atlas/importers/atlas_freshness_monitor.py'; h=hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest(); sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+h) if h!='1603897848172f58ba294c017e53d9f1b3bf87ba6ef33984d4b3fd675809cfb8' else print('fresh sha ok')"

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS per-source OUTPUT-freshness monitor (catches stopped-producing + dead timers)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
Nice=8
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/audit.env
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/importers/atlas_freshness_monitor.py
TimeoutStartSec=300
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=30

[Unit]
Description=ATLAS freshness monitor every 15min

[Timer]
OnBootSec=90s
OnUnitActiveSec=15min
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import subprocess as s; s.run(['systemctl','daemon-reload']); s.run(['systemctl','enable','--now','atlas-freshness.timer']); s.run(['systemctl','reset-failed','atlas-freshness.service']); s.run(['systemctl','start','--no-block','atlas-freshness.service']); print('freshness monitor live')"
